cards.title: Local files are the primary memory layer
cards.applies_when: The task involves projects, rules, memory, file paths, or prior decisions.
do: Read AGENTS.md, rule cards, project cards, task memory, and error routes first.
Treat Markdown and JSON files as the current local source of truth.
Verify current APIs, prices, laws, and platform rules from official sources when needed.
dont: Do not continue long-running projects only from the active chat context.
Do not treat old chat history or external notes as current rules without checking local state.

cards.title: Use three-layer recall before complex work
cards.applies_when: The task involves project handoff, technical decisions, external knowledge, or current facts.
do: Recall L1 project memory and rules first.
Recall L2 local external knowledge when background material is needed.
Use L3 real-time verification for APIs, prices, versions, laws, platform rules, and security guidance.
dont: Do not mix personal rules, external evidence, and real-time facts into one undifferentiated layer.
Do not let a real-time source automatically overwrite local project decisions.

cards.title: Record failed routes
cards.scope: failed attempts, rework, dead ends, risky approaches
cards.applies_when: A task reveals a path that failed, caused rework, wasted time, or created risk.
do: Write wrong_path, why_failed, corrected_path, and next_prevention to error_routes.md.
Check error routes before similar future tasks.
Promote repeated error routes into rule cards or SOP constraints.
dont: Do not leave failed paths only in the current chat.
Do not keep disproven approaches as active recommended routes.

cards.title: Public docs use English; machine fields stay stable
cards.scope: public documentation, code, data, and templates
cards.applies_when: Writing README, docs, JSON, scripts, API fields, or environment variables.
do: Use clear English for public human-facing docs in this template.
Keep variables, JSON keys, commands, environment variables, and API fields in English.
dont: Do not translate machine-readable keys or package names.
Do not mix private personal language rules into the public template unless documented as a customization.

cards.title: Traceability over cleverness
cards.scope: long-term conclusions, project state, rule changes, deliverables
cards.applies_when: Producing conclusions, updating memory, or claiming completion.
do: Keep source_trace, file paths, validation method, and update time.
Distinguish created, ran, verified, reusable, and publish_ready states.
dont: Do not replace evidence with vague confidence.
Do not call a path runnable just because it exists.

cards.title: Write only reusable memory
cards.scope: long-term memory and immediate task memory
cards.applies_when: Preparing to write task results, preferences, project state, or external conclusions into memory.
do: Write long-term preferences, stable rules, project state, important decisions, reusable flows, and verification results.
Check traceability, actionability, stability, freshness, and safety before writing.
dont: Do not store full private chat transcripts.
Do not write one-off emotion, temporary guesses, or unverified conclusions.

cards.title: Exclude secrets from memory text
cards.scope: all files, logs, project cards, and dashboard data
cards.applies_when: Content involves passwords, API keys, tokens, cookies, verification codes, private keys, customer data, or full private transcripts.
do: Record only sensitivity, secret_ref, purpose, exclusion reason, and sanitized source_trace.
Treat uncertain material as more sensitive until reviewed.
dont: Do not store keys, cookies, tokens, verification codes, or private raw transcripts in any formal file.
Do not repeat secret plaintext in logs or dashboards.

cards.title: Long-running work needs a project card
cards.scope: projects, systems, features, automations, content assets, and research tasks
cards.applies_when: A task spans more than one session, has multiple stages, needs future handoff, or needs acceptance criteria.
do: Create at least project_brief.md, current_context.md, progress_log.md, evidence_trace.md, and acceptance_checklist.md.
Keep handoff indexes short; store detailed project state inside project cards.
dont: Do not leave a long-running project only in chat history.
Do not pollute long-term rules with one-off project details.

cards.title: External material is evidence first
cards.scope: docs, repos, papers, tutorials, community experience, API docs, and news
cards.applies_when: Adding, reading, distilling, or citing external material.
do: Keep raw, manifest, index, digest, analysis, candidate_rule, and active_rule separate.
Adapt external conclusions locally before promoting them to rules.
dont: Do not copy raw external material directly into long-term rules.
Do not let external material automatically override current project rules.

cards.title: High-change facts require real-time verification
cards.scope: APIs, models, prices, laws, policies, platform rules, software versions, security guidance, market news
cards.applies_when: The task depends on an external fact that may have changed.
do: Prefer official docs, official repos, or primary sources.
Record checked_at, sources, current_facts, and impact_on_local_rules.
Only overwrite old facts at the current-fact layer; rule changes still need local adaptation.
dont: Do not answer current facts from old local digests.
Do not turn real-time search results directly into long-term rules.

cards.title: SOP before automation
cards.scope: repeatable processes, scripts, scheduled tasks, and workflows
cards.applies_when: Considering whether to turn a manual process into a tool or automation.
do: Automate only after inputs, steps, outputs, and acceptance criteria are stable.
Prefer automation for low-risk, retryable, observable processes with clear human checkpoints.
dont: Do not automate deletion, overwrites, publishing, payments, permissions, or secret reads by default.
Do not hand open-ended judgment to automation without acceptance criteria.

cards.title: Auto-evolution must be controlled
cards.scope: rule upgrades, SOP upgrades, scripts, memory demotion, and system cleanup
cards.applies_when: A task, weekly audit, or error review reveals reusable knowledge.
do: Write new facts to immediate task memory and project cards first.
Write failed routes to error_routes.md.
Promote repeated successful flows to SOPs before scripts.
Demote stale or failed rules to review, deprecated, or archived.
dont: Do not automatically delete the only evidence.
Do not promote a one-off guess into a long-term rule.
Do not automate publishing, payment, permissions, or secrets.
